title: Randorn 2

{{Quote|These mythological incarnations of destruction might pose a real threat if the species were to expand beyond the remote confines of Randorn 2.|An entry, dedicated to the ibliton, of a holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
'''Randorn 2''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] astronomical object situated in the [[Rolion sector/Legends|Rolion sector]]. Its surface featured [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]], in which the caustic [[harrowbane]] [[Plant/Legends|plant]] grew and which were home to the large [[Mollusk/Legends|mollusk]] called the [[ibliton]]. Iblitons were apex predators and held a significant role in the mythology of Randorn 2's [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Mizx]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who believed the creatures were incarnations of an evil [[Hershoon the Destroyer|mage]]. At some point by the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], a group of [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologists]] visited Randorn 2, where they fought and [[Death/Legends|killed]] an ibliton attacking a Mizx village.

==Description==
{{Quote|No other beast of the Randorn bayous can defeat a full-grown ibliton.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
Randorn 2, also known simply as Randorn, was a remote celestial body<ref name="Creatures" /> located in the [[Randorn system/Legends|Randorn system]],<ref name="TEA" /> a part of the [[Rolion sector/Legends|Rolion sector]]<ref name="Rolion" /> in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA" /> Its surface featured [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]] and [[Water/Legends|bayous]], which were [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to at least one type of [[Tree/Legends|tree]] as well as the caustic [[Plant/Legends|weed]] known as [[harrowbane]]. The astronomical object's fauna included large [[Bird/Legends|avians]] and [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] and small [[Fish/Legends|fish]] and [[Rodent/Legends|rodents]]. Two specific types of creature native to Randorn 2 were the [[hecxt]] and [[layall]].<ref name="Creatures" />

At the top of the [[Diet/Legends|food]] chain in Randorn 2's swamps was the [[ibliton]], a two- to five-[[Meter/Legends|meters]] long, slow, predatory<ref name="Creatures" /> [[Mollusk/Legends|mollusk]]<ref name="CSWE ibliton">{{CSWECite|II|74|ibliton}}</ref> with characteristics of both [[Cephalopod/Legends|cephalopods]] and [[Crustacean/Legends|crustaceans]]. It had a hard shell that protected its soft inner body from both predators and the harsh conditions of its swamp habitat, and as part of its [[Reproduction/Legends|reproductive]] cycle, the creature would deposit one [[Egg/Legends|egg]] in the swamp waters, which would then activate fertilization compounds in the egg.<ref name="Creatures" />

==History==
{{Quote|The theory ''[sic]'', put forth by Harzoon Parr of the Galactic History Foundation, that iblitons were 'planted' on Randorn 2 by some lost starfaring race have interested xenobiologists but remain pure speculation.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
[[File:Randorn_system.jpg|thumb|right|300px|During the Clone Wars, Randorn 2 was situated outside of the borders of the Galactic Republic and the Confederacy of Independent Systems.]]
Fossil record indicated that iblitons lived on Randorn 2 at least as long as [[600,000 BBY|six hundred thousand]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to<ref name="Creatures" /> the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="CSWE ibliton" /> and they predated the civilization of the celestial body's [[Mizx]] [[Species/Legends|species]] by [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]].<ref name="Creatures" /> The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Clone Captain/Legends|clone trooper captain]] [[Rex/Legends|Rex]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] was familiar with the slow-moving mollusks of Randorn 2<ref name="TCW">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' novelization]]</ref> by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].<ref name="22 BBY">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of the [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' novelization]] to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] of 22 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]]–[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the celestial body was situated outside of the territories of both the Republic and its enemy, the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="TEA" />

At some point, [[Harzoon Parr]] of the [[Galactic History Foundation]] formulated a hypothesis that the iblitons had been introduced on Randorn 2 by a since-vanished [[Spacefaring/Legends|spacefaring]] culture; a possibility that remained speculative yet fascinated [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologists]]. A group of researchers of the [[Tyberious Institute of Xenobiology]] at one point visited Randorn 2, where they dredged millennia-old ibliton shells from the celestial body's swamps. The researchers were also present at a Mizx village when an ibliton attacked it. Ultimately, the xenobiologists were able to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the creature, but not before one Mizx had also perished. The researcher [[Meslinger]] subsequently witnessed the native's [[Funeral/Legends|funeral]], and at some point by the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], an entry of a [[Holo-brochure|holobrochure]] for the [[Tagge Holographic Museum]] of<ref name="Creatures" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Chandrila">{{CSWECite|I|119|Chandrila}}</ref> [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] was dedicated to the ibliton and provided information on the creature, including its role in Randorn 2's ecosystem. The brochure also noted that the ibliton could pose a serious threat if it ever spread off-world.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Mizx}}
{{Quote|In the swamps of Randorn 2, the ibliton stands at the top of the food chain and occupies a significant role in local mythology.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
Randorn 2 was home to the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] Mizx species. Technologically relatively primitive, the Mizx lived in villages and kept domesticated hecxts and layalls. The ibliton was an important element of the Mizx mythology, being viewed as an avatar of the deceased evil [[Magic/Legends|mage]] [[Hershoon the Destroyer]]. Mizx [[Youngling/Legends|children]] learned early of the dangers associated with venturing into Randorn 2's swamps,<ref name="Creatures" /> and the natives planted harrowbane around their villages due to believing that the plant, which they considered sacred, warded off iblitons.<ref name="CSWE harrowbane">{{CSWECite|II|22|harrowbane}}</ref> Nevertheless, three published accounts told about ibliton raids on Mizx settlements.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Behind the scenes==
Randorn 2 was created by [[Phil Brucato]] for the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the celestial body was depicted in an illustration by [[David Plunkett]].<ref name="Creatures" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Randorn [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Randorn 2, in grid square Q-6.<ref name="TEA" /> The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to that book later placed the system in the [[Belderone sector/Legends|Belderone sector]];<ref name="EAOC Randorn">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Randorn system/Legends|Randorn system]]</ref> however, [https://web.archive.org/web/20180325111127/https://www.starwars.com/news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors] feature of the Online Companion depicted the territory of that [[Sector/Legends|sector]]<ref name="EAOC Outer Rim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> and the location of the Randorn system—as shown on a map in ''The Essential Atlas''<ref name="TEA" />—as not intersecting. Instead, the feature depicted the location of Randorn 2 as falling within the territory of the Rolion sector, which this article assumes is correct.<ref name="EAOC Outer Rim" />
